title: Why the SpaceX IPO Will Affect Your 401(k), Like It or Not
summary: Elon Musk’s rocket company, on the cusp of the largest initial public offering ever, will soon end up in index funds after rule changes by Nasdaq and other index providers.

title: Gwynne Shotwell, Elon Musk’s No. 2 at SpaceX, Is the Company’s Steady Hand
summary: Gwynne Shotwell, the president and chief operating officer, is the adult-in-the-room foil to Mr. Musk as SpaceX prepares for a blockbuster initial public offering.

title: Who’s Excited for SpaceX’s I.P.O.? Space Nerds.
summary: Few are as delighted by the pending market debut as the vibrant community of space enthusiasts who want to push discovery of the final frontier.

title: Hot I.P.O. Summer + What Is A.I. Doing to Math? + HatGPT
summary: “We are on track to see what might be the three biggest I.P.O.s of all time.”

title: People Love to Hate This Fake Private Equity Guy
summary: Johnny Hilbrant Partridge has found social media fame as PE Guy, a character who embodies obnoxious entitlement.

title: SpaceX Has $30 Billion Deal to Provide Google With A.I. Computing Power
summary: Elon Musk’s rocket company said Google would pay it $920 million a month, as it prepared for its initial public offering.

title: Reid Hoffman to Leave Microsoft’s Board of Directors
summary: The LinkedIn co-founder was a key bridge to Microsoft’s relationship with OpenAI, but he also came with some baggage.

title: From Cow-Milking Robots to Weed-Zapping Lasers, Farmers Are Embracing A.I.
summary: We asked three farmers to tell us how new technology is revolutionizing the way they work.

title: Wary of U.S., Carney Bets on AI Strategy for Canada
summary: The country on Thursday released a national artificial intelligence strategy that focuses on building its sovereign capability and protecting consumers.

title: What Are A.I. Agents Actually Doing?
summary: A San Francisco start-up called Arena found that people are most likely to use A.I. agents on the job, particularly if they are in the tech industry.

title: How LinkedIn Found Its Social Platform Era
summary: Celebrities and paid influencers have transformed the career-focused platform, which for some users, has become a job unto itself.

title: The Do’s and Don’ts of Buying Used Tech Gadgets
summary: With a shortage of some components driving up costs of consumer electronics, refurbished devices are an attractive alternative to buying new.

title: The Small-Business Owners Managing Whole Armies of A.I. Employees
summary: When you turn A.I. agents loose on your finances, email and customers, what could possibly go wrong?

title: Can These ChatGPT Ads Make You Love A.I.?
summary: A campaign for ChatGPT relies on heartwarming retro vibes to sell a product that has become a source of concern for most Americans.

title: ‘Teachers Are Going to Hate It’: How Social Media Apps Hooked Teens at School
summary: Internal documents show how tech giants grabbed children’s attention throughout the day, a strategy that schools say has undermined education.

title: SpaceX IPO to Be Largest Ever at $135 Share Price
summary: The $135 share price means Elon Musk’s rocket maker is poised to exceed the 2019 initial public offering of Saudi Aramco in both valuation and money raised.

title: European Union Outlines Plan to Reduce Dependence on American Tech
summary: The 27-nation European Union outlined how it hopes to expand the region’s data centers, semiconductors and cloud computing capabilities.

title: Scientists Find Way to Supercharge Dangerous Computer ‘Worms’ With A.I.
summary: Researchers at the University of Toronto showed how hackers could use artificial intelligence to create a program that could target any known flaw in the world’s computers.

title: Trump Signs Executive Order Seeking Oversight of A.I. Models
summary: The order, which signaled a shift from the hands-off approach the White House had previously taken toward A.I., followed debates over how to gain control of A.I. models without disrupting innovation.

title: Meta Expands Safety Features for Teenagers
summary: The changes, after Meta’s legal losses in two child safety cases, are aimed at limiting harmful content shown to teenagers on Instagram, Facebook and Messenger.

title: Florida Sues OpenAI Over Chatbot Safety Concerns
summary: The state became the first to sue the ChatGPT maker over claims that its technology posed a risk to children and that the company had failed to warn the public of dangers.

title: Anthropic Files to Go Public, Setting Stage for Huge I.P.O.
summary: The artificial intelligence company, which is racing OpenAI to the stock market, has seen explosive growth over the last year thanks largely to technology that can automatically write computer code.

title: Is A.I. Replacing Tech Workers or Providing an Excuse for Job Cuts?
summary: Tech industry layoffs are accelerating, and executives have been quick to say it’s because their companies are doing more with artificial intelligence, even when there may be more to it.

title: How Box Created 13 New Types of Jobs Because of A.I.
summary: Box, a Silicon Valley software maker, expects to have more employees, not fewer, as it hires A.I. architects, A.I. solutions managers and other new A.I.-related positions.

title: Chinese Military Sought Nvidia Chips for Years, Report Says
summary: An analysis of six years of procurement records suggests that the People’s Liberation Army has openly tried to acquire restricted U.S. technology.

title: China Aims A.I. at Predicting Who Could Pose a Political Risk
summary: New research examines how a Chinese company struggled to develop its predictive surveillance technology while U.S. restrictions were in place.

title: Nvidia Has a Plan to Put Its Chips in Personal Computers
summary: The world’s most valuable company is chasing Intel and Apple as it tries to bring A.I. agents to laptops and desktops.
